title: Sarga 80
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having narrated this to Rama, the great sage Agastya then began another part of the same story.” and develops toward “And O one with faultless limbs, my father will give me to you when he is requested.”

This passage begins with “Having thus gone under the sway of desire, Danda, intoxicated with passion, replied to Araja, placing his folded hands on his head.” and develops toward “Arajā, greatly terrified and weeping, waited for her god-like father not far from the hermitage. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये…”

verses.sanskrit: एतदाख्याय रामाय महर्षिः कुम्भसम्भवः । अस्यामेवापरं वाक्यं कथायामुपचक्रमे ।। 7.80.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having narrated this to Rama, the great sage Agastya then began another part of the same story.

verses.sanskrit: ततः स दण्डः काकुत्स्थ बहुवर्षगणायुतम् । अकरोत्तत्र दान्तात्मा राज्यं निहतकण्टकम् ।। 7.80.2 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, then King Danda, with a controlled mind, ruled his kingdom there, free from enemies, for many thousands of years.

verses.sanskrit: अथ काले तु कस्मिंश्चिद्राजा भार्गवमाश्रमम् । रमणीयमुपाक्रामच्चैत्रे मासि मनोरमे ।। 7.80.3 ।।
verses.translation: Then, at some point, the king approached the delightful hermitage of Bhrigu in the charming month of Chaitra.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र भार्गवकन्यां स रूपेणाप्रतिमां भुवि । विचरन्तीं वनोद्देशे दण्डो ऽपश्यदनुत्तमाम् ।। 7.80.4 ।।
verses.translation: There, King Danda saw the unsurpassed daughter of Bhrigu, unequalled in beauty on earth, wandering in the forest region.

verses.sanskrit: स दृष्ट्वा तां सुदुर्मेधा अनङ्गशरपीडितः । अभिगम्य सुसंविग्नां कन्यां वचनमब्रवीत् ।। 7.80.5 ।।
verses.translation: Having seen her, the very foolish king, afflicted by the arrows of Kama, approached the very agitated maiden and spoke words to her.

verses.sanskrit: कुतस्त्वमसि सुश्रोणि कस्य वा ऽसि सुता शुभे । पीडितो ऽहमनङ्गेन गच्छामि त्वां शुभानने ।। 7.80.6 ।।
verses.translation: O beautiful-hipped one, from where are you, or whose daughter are you, O auspicious one; I am tormented by Cupid, and so I approach you, O beautiful-faced one.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य त्वेवं ब्रुवाणस्य मोहोन्मत्तस्य कामिनः । भार्गवी प्रत्युवाचेदं वचः सानुनयं नृपम् ।। 7.80.7 ।।
verses.translation: To that lustful king, who was speaking thus, maddened by infatuation, the daughter of Bhrigu replied with these humble words.

verses.sanskrit: भार्गवस्य सुतां विद्धि देवस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः । अरजां नाम राजेन्द्र ज्येष्ठामाश्रमवासिनीम् ।। 7.80.8 ।।
verses.translation: O king of kings, know me to be Araja by name, the eldest daughter of the divine sage Bhrigu, whose deeds are pure, and who dwells in the hermitage.

verses.sanskrit: मा मां स्पृश बलाद्राजन्कन्या पितृवशा ह्यहम् । गुरुः पिता मे राजेन्द्र त्वं च शिष्यो महात्मनः । व्यसनं सुमहत्क्रुद्धः स ते दद्यान्महातपाः ।। 7.80.9 ।।
verses.translation: O king, do not touch me by force, for I am a maiden dependent on my father; O king of kings, my father is your preceptor, and you are the disciple of that great-souled one, and if enraged, that sage of great penance would inflict a very great calamity upon you.

verses.sanskrit: यदि वान्यन्मया कार्यं धर्मदृष्टेन सत्पथा । वरयस्व नरश्रेष्ठ पितरं मे महाद्युतिम् ।। 7.80.10 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, if anything else is to be done by me according to the righteous path of dharma, then ask for me from my glorious father.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यथा तु फलं तुभ्यं भवेद्घोराभिसंहितम् । क्रोधेन हि पिता मे ऽसौ त्रैलोक्यमपि निर्दहेत् ।। 7.80.11 ।।
verses.translation: Otherwise, the consequence for you would be terrible, for my father, in his anger, could indeed burn down even the three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: दास्यते चानवद्याङ्ग तव मा याचितः पिता ।। 7.80.12 ।।
verses.translation: And O one with faultless limbs, my father will give me to you when he is requested.

verses.sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवाणामरजां दण्डः कामवशं गतः । प्रत्युवाच मदोन्मत्तः शिरस्याधाय चाञ्जलिम् ।। 7.80.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus gone under the sway of desire, Danda, intoxicated with passion, replied to Araja, placing his folded hands on his head.

verses.sanskrit: प्रसादं कुरु सुश्रोणि न कालं क्षेप्तुमर्हसि । त्वत्कृते हि मम प्राणा विदीर्यन्ते वरानने ।। 7.80.14 ।।
verses.translation: O one with beautiful hips and a beautiful face, please grant me your favor; you should not waste time, for indeed, my life breaths are being torn apart for your sake.

verses.sanskrit: त्वां प्राप्य मे वधो वा स्याच्छापो वा यदि दारुणः । भक्तं भजस्व मां भीरु भजमानं सुविह्वलम् ।। 7.80.15 ।।
verses.translation: O timid one, even if I obtain you and face death or a terrible curse, please accept me, your devoted servant who is greatly agitated.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु तां कन्यां दोर्भ्यां गृह्य बलाद्बली । विस्फुरन्तीं यथाकामं मैथुनायोपचक्रमे ।। 7.80.16 ।।
verses.translation: Having spoken thus, the powerful Daṇḍa seized the struggling maiden with his arms by force and proceeded to have sexual intercourse with her as he desired.

verses.sanskrit: एतमर्थं महाघोरं दण्डः कृत्वा सुदारुणम् । नगरं प्रययावाशु मधुमन्तमनुत्तमम् ।। 7.80.17 ।।
verses.translation: Having committed this extremely dreadful and very cruel act, Daṇḍa quickly departed to his unequalled city of Madhumanta.

verses.sanskrit: अरजा ऽपि रुदन्ती सा आश्रमस्याविदूरतः । प्रतीक्षन्ति सुसंत्रस्ता पितरं देवसन्निभम् ।। 7.80.18 ।।
verses.translation: Arajā, greatly terrified and weeping, waited for her god-like father not far from the hermitage. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे ऽशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 80 ।। Thus ends the eightieth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
